characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: A shady businessman and conman, eternally involved in dodgy dealings andget-rich-quick schemes. Breakout Character: Started out as a secondary character to Terry, but by the third series their roles were equal, and gradually he began to usurp Terry's role. While he never publicly expressed dissatisfaction with the situation, Dennis Waterman admitted that he did often think that the later seasons should have changed the name of the show to reflect that they were more about Arthur than his minder. Characterisation Marches On: Began as a somewhat more dangerous and higher-level criminal before developing into the "alright" shady rogue. Cigar Chomper: Arthur is usually seen smoking Castella Panatella cigars, as part of his superficial image of wealth and status. Con Man: Regularly sees an opportunity to make money off a situation.

characters.name: Arthur Daley
characters.description: A shady businessman and conman, eternally involved in dodgy dealings andget-rich-quick schemes. Breakout Character: Started out as a secondary character to Terry, but by the third series their roles were equal, and gradually he began to usurp Terry's role. While he never publicly expressed dissatisfaction with the situation, Dennis Waterman admitted that he did often think that the later seasons should have changed the name of the show to reflect that they were more about Arthur than his minder. Characterisation Marches On: Began as a somewhat more dangerous and higher-level criminal before developing into the "alright" shady rogue. Cigar Chomper: Arthur is usually seen smoking Castella Panatella cigars, as part of his superficial image of wealth and status. Con Man: Regularly sees an opportunity to make money off a situation.

characters.name: TerryMcCann
characters.description: A likable and scrupulous retired boxer, who has become bodyguard ("minder") to Arthur. Book Dumb: Terry isn't quite aGenius Bruiser, but he's certainly more intelligent than most characters. Bruiser with a Soft Center: He's the moral compass of the show and is shown to be pretty kind and caring at points. Butt-Monkey: Regularly suffers comedically from Arthur's manipulation, though he nearly always comes out on top in the end. Commitment Issues: None of his relationships with women last, in part because he cannot break Arthur's hold on him which he views as more important.

characters.name: Ray Daley
characters.description: Arthur's young nephew, who he employs as his replacement minder when he sees that he can handle himself in a tough situation. Bruiser with a Soft Center: To a lesser extent than Terry, but still there. Convenient Replacement Character: Arrives just after Terry leaves to move to Australia. Good Old Fisticuffs: Surprisingly, Ray is just as good at Terry at fighting. Only Sane Man: In some episodes.

characters.name: Dave Harris
characters.description: Arthur's childhood friend and owner of the Winchester Club. And Starring: In the closing credits of series 3, we get "And Glynn Edwards as Dave". From series 4 to series 6, it's "With Glynn Edwards as Dave" on the guest stars list in the opening credits. A Day in the Limelight: Of a kind in the "Number" promotional video for Premium Bonds, as the plot centres around the Winchester Club rather than Arthur or Terry. Breakout Character: In the first series, he was only in half the episodes and barely had more than one scene in each. He quickly caught on with viewers and his role was enlarged significantly to the point that most would consider him just as important as Arthur and Terry to the show. The Confidant: Frequently this to Arthur and Terry, and can be relied on to get them out of a bad place as a last resort.

characters.name: "'Er Indoors"
characters.description: All in the Manual/Named In The Adaptation: She's named Sarah in Leon Griffiths'Mindernovel. The Ghost: We hear about her all the time but she's never seen on-screen. She Who Must Not Be Seen: Arthur's occasional comments about her are all that give viewers a mental image as to the personality of his unseen wife.

characters.name: DS Albert "Cheerful Charlie" Chisholm
characters.description: A local policeman who has dedicated his entire career to attempting to arrest Arthur. Affectionate Nickname: "Cheerful Charlie", given to him by Arthur and the other Winchester regulars. Breakout Character: In the first two series, his role was much smaller. He became increasingly used and involved as the show shifted towards focusing on Arthur and his dodgy deals. Put on a Bus: At the start of series 7, he's written out by quitting the force to become a security guard. Rabid Cop: Brought to a nervous breakdown by his pursuit of Arthur and Terry's schemes.

characters.name: DC/DS Ronald Rycott
characters.description: A corrupt police officer regularly determined to catch Arthur. Alliterative Name: Ronald Rycott. Breakout Character: In the first two series, his role was much smaller. He became increasingly used and involved as the show shifted towards focusing on Arthur and his dodgy deals. Chuck Cunningham Syndrome: Along with DC Mellish, he doesn't appear in the Ray episodes, with no explanation for his absence. Probably because Peter Childs had died inbetween series 7 and 8. Dirty Cop: Often moonlights when off-duty.

characters.name: DC/DS "Taff" Jones
characters.description: Chisholm's deputy and sidekick, quietly amused by Arthur's humiliation of Chisholm. Beleaguered Assistant: To Chisholm. It's obvious that he barely tolerates his put-downs, but stays with him in fear of unemployment. Chuck Cunningham Syndrome: Along with DCMcDonald, he doesn't appear in the Ray episodes, with no explanation for his absence. Punch-Clock Villain: Only attempts to arrest Arthur and Terry because it's his job, and has a friendlier relationship with them than the other officers. When off duty, he sometimes has a drink at the Winchester with Arthur and Terry. Rank Up: Promoted to a Detective Sergeant after Chisholm left.
